title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Bella Goddard informs Corrodick that her sister-in-law, Sonya, had a rift with Randall and eventually moved to America with twin children named Pip and Emma. Bella explains that she suggested Mr. Goddard include Sonya's children in his will to ensure they would inherit his property if Bella passed away before Sonya. Corrodick suspects that these twins, potentially living in Chipping Cheddar, may be seeking the inheritance by tracking the family's wealth and the status of Letitia Blacklock. As Bella grows tired, she urgently pleads with Corrodick to protect Blanche, warning that she is in danger.

After Corrodick leaves the room, a MacLane nurse informs him that Bella's personal documents and photos were destroyed during the Blitz in London. Corrodick continues to ponder the motive of the twins, theorizing that they might be penniless individuals using false identities to target the Blacklock estate.

Meanwhile, at a small estate, Letitia Blacklock instructs the cook, Midge, to prepare various sandwiches and a specialty cake for Dora Bunner's birthday celebration. Midge, a Central European, expresses anxiety about the house and discusses the ingredients for a heavy, chocolate-frosted cake. Despite Midge's superstition regarding the word "die"—referencing a comment by Mr. Patrick about her cake being the "death of deliciousness"—Letitia reassures her. In the hallway, Letitia prevents Dora from entering the kitchen to avoid disturbing Midge's mood. Dora mentions that Edmund Swithinham called to wish her a happy birthday and intends to bring honey as a gift.
